column2: A technician has arranged the conductors in a twisted-pair cable and inserted them into a compatible modular plug. Which tool completes the termination?
column4:
Why

The crimper presses the plug contacts into the conductors and secures the plug to the cable jacket.

Exam Trap

A punchdown tool seats individual wires into a wiring block; it does not attach a modular plug.

column2: Users report unreliable Wi-Fi in one area. Which tool should a technician use for 802.11 analysis to identify wireless errors or interference?
column4:
Why

A Wi-Fi analyzer examines 802.11 activity, errors, and interference; a cable tester cannot reveal the wireless environment.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.20; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.8
column9: A+::220-1201::2.8 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingTools Basic HighYield WiFiAnalyzer WirelessTroubleshooting Source::Messer-v170

column2: An unlabeled wall cable enters a bundle of many cables in a wiring room. Which tool pair should a technician use to identify the matching cable?
column4:
Why

The generator places a tone on the cable, and the probe detects that tone at the matching run or termination.

Exam Trap

A cable tester checks wiring continuity after both ends are known; it is not the primary tool for locating an unknown run.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.20; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.8
column9: A+::220-1201::2.8 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingTools Basic HighYield TonerProbe CableTracing Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which tool should a technician use to seat individual wires into a 66 or 110 wiring block?
column4:
Why

The tool punches each wire into the wiring block and commonly trims the excess wire during the punch.

Exam Trap

A crimper attaches a plug to a cable; a punchdown tool is used on wiring blocks.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.20; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.8
column9: A+::220-1201::2.8 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingTools Basic HighYield PunchdownTool StructuredCabling Source::Messer-v170

column2: A newly terminated network cable may have an open conductor or crossed conductors. Which tool should the technician use first?
column4:
Why

A basic cable tester checks continuity and conductor order, making it appropriate for these wiring faults.

Tech Wisdom

Basic continuity testing does not certify the cable's full signal performance.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.20; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.8
column9: A+::220-1201::2.8 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingTools Basic HighYield CableTester Continuity Source::Messer-v170

column2: A technician needs to send a copy of traffic from a live network link to packet-capture equipment. Which physical tool is designed for this purpose?
column4:
Why

A physical tap sits in the traffic path and exposes a copy of the traffic to monitoring equipment.

Exam Trap

A loopback plug tests one port's local transmit/receive path; it does not copy traffic from a live link.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.20; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 2.8
column9: A+::220-1201::2.8 A+::220-1201::Domain2-Networking A+::220-1201::NetworkingTools Basic NetworkTap TrafficCapture Source::Messer-v170
